title: `$fwrite` doesn't write `'\0'` chars
body:
When writing a const `8'h00` into a `%c` format specifier in `$fwrite`, the `'\0'` is not included in the generated string.

From the reproduction below, we can see that the zeros are not included in the generated C++ code:

## Reproduction steps

With this `repro.sv`:

```systemverilog
module test;
integer f;

initial begin
	f=$fopen("zeros", "wb");
	$fwrite(f, "hello1%c2%c%c3%c%c%c4", 8'h00, 8'h00, 8'h00, 8'h00, 8'h00, 8'h00);
	$fclose(f);
end

endmodule
```

Run:

```sh
verilator repro.sv --cc && grep WRITEF -r obj_dir/
```

### Observed behavior

```sh
$ verilator repro.sv --cc && grep WRITEF -r obj_dir/
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.036 2025-04-27 rev v5.036
- Verilator: Built from 0.000 MB sources in 0 modules, into 0.000 MB in 0 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 0.000 s (elab=0.000, cvt=0.000, bld=0.000); cpu 0.000 s on 1 threads; alloced 7.961 MB
obj_dir/Vrepro___024root__DepSet_hb41e4d0c__0__Slow.cpp:    VL_FWRITEF_NX(test__DOT__f,"hello1234",0);
```

### Expected behavior

With the problem fixed the output should be:

```sh
$ ../verilator/bin/verilator repro.sv --cc && grep WRITEF -r obj_dir/
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.039 devel rev v5.038-106-g9d38e6326 (mod)
- Verilator: Built from 0.023 MB sources in 2 modules, into 0.018 MB in 6 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 0.019 s (elab=0.002, cvt=0.009, bld=0.000); cpu 0.010 s on 1 threads; alloced 20.141 MB
obj_dir/Vrepro___024root__DepSet_hb41e4d0c__0__Slow.cpp:    VL_FWRITEF_NX(test__DOT__f,"hello1\0002\000\0003\000\000\0004"s,0);
```

## Related Issues

The issue is similar to #4862, but with `%c` instead of `%u`.
